vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi palette that balances Japanese minimalism and Scandinavian comfort. Use low-profile furniture, warm woods, woven textures and soft ambient light. Prioritize open sightlines, decluttered surfaces and a small dedicated work corner. Keep scale human and kid-safe: rounded edges, low heights, and natural materials.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals and soft earth tones (creamy off-white base, honey oak midtones, muted caramel and warm greys) support both a serene Japandi look and family-friendly wear. Accent with charcoal for contrast and natural greenery for liveliness.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, warm ambient light with layered sources: diffuse daylight where available, warm dimmable overhead pendants in dining, low floor and table lamps in living and bedroom, and a warm task lamp at the desk. Keep color temperature ~2700–3000K for intimacy.
materials: linen and wool textiles (cushions, curtains, rugs)
matte black metal for small accents and lamp arms
natural woven rugs (jute or tatami-edge blends)
textures: soft looped wool rugs with low pile
furniture_language: rounded corners for child-safety and calm silhouette
slim, tapered legs and cantilevered elements
single-statement pendant in dining; sculptural tabletop centerpieces
styling_rules: Keep surfaces mostly clear — 1–3 curated objects per surface (vase, tray, lamp).
Use layered rugs to define low seating and tea-nook moments.
Balance negative space with singular sculptural pieces — no gallery walls.
Introduce plants in odd numbers and near windows or corners for scale.
Prioritize durable, washable textiles in family zones; avoid pure white upholstery.
avoid: high-gloss finishes, chrome or flashy metal
overfilling the floorplan with large, tall furniture
busy patterned fabrics and excessive wall art
fragile glass decor at child height

room_guidance.headline: Low-profile family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Anchor the Nest with a low, light-oak sofa on a textured wool rug oriented towards the patio openings. Place a compact, wall-aligned standing desk in a corner near the large glass door so it benefits from daylight but does not block circulation to the patio. Include a low coffee table, one lounge chair or pouf for flexible seating, and a small play basket stored by the sofa. Maintain clear pathway to patio door.
must_include: compact standing_desk (folding/leaning type) near patio glazing
one child-safe pouf and a woven toy basket
must_avoid: oversized sectional that blocks circulation
tall freestanding shelving between sofa and patio door
fragile decor on low coffee table

room_guidance.headline: Serene bedroom with adjacent crib and modest storage
room_guidance.guidance: Compose the Dream as a calm sleeping zone: low platform bed centered on the longest wall, narrow dresser or built-in closet for clothing, and the crib positioned to keep a clear path to the door and closet. Keep decor minimal: one low bedside table lamp, a soft wool rug, and a small plant near the window. Ensure crib placement meets safety (clear of direct draft from window).
must_include: low platform bed (queen) with warm-wood frame
crib (placed with 36" minimum clearance from main walking path)
soft bedside lamp and low-pile rug
must_avoid: placing crib under a directly drafty window
large, high dressers that block closet access
heavy decorative lighting over the crib

room_guidance.headline: Compact, uncluttered kitchen with open flow to dining
room_guidance.guidance: Stage the kitchen as efficient and tidy: minimal counter clutter, warm oak or matte-lacquer lower cabinets, open shelving for a few ceramics, and a small herb pot by the sink. Use under-cabinet task lighting to suggest usable workspaces. Maintain a clear visual connection to the Dine area.
must_include: stack of neutral ceramic bowls on open shelf
matte cabinet finishes in warm neutral
must_avoid: full countertop of appliances or utensils
large island that would crowd the dining nook

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Given the small footprint, use a round dining table (36–42 in) to maximize circulation. Center a woven rattan pendant over the table, and stage with 3–4 linen-upholstered chairs. A single stoneware vase with a dried grass stem creates an elegant focal point. Keep the bay/window seating clear to emphasize natural light.
must_avoid: rectangular large dining table
excessive place settings or table clutter

room_guidance.headline: Covered outdoor extension with low-seat tea nook feel
room_guidance.guidance: Stage Relax as a sheltered, low-slung seating area connected visually to the Nest and Dine. Use a low teak or oak bench, floor cushions or poufs, and a natural-fiber rug. Include a lantern-style floor lamp or string lights for evening warmth. Keep plantings minimal and wind-safe.
must_include: low wooden bench or tea-table with floor cushions
one weather-resistant lantern or string light
must_avoid: large, heavy outdoor sofas that block openings
fragile decor exposed to elements

warnings: Dining footprint (≈7.8 x 8 ft) restricts table diameter; recommend round table ≤42 in and avoid sideboards.
Standing desk in living must not block egress to patio — keep desk against wall or windowlight-adjacent corner.
Bedroom crib placement should respect minimum clearances and not obstruct closet or door swing.

overall_mood: Calm, warm, minimal — family-ready Japandi with soft daylight and layered natural textures.
global_style_notes: Keep silhouettes low and horizontal to emphasize Japanese influence and visual calm.
Use warm oak as the primary wood tone; repeat in furniture legs and small millwork for cohesion.
Limit decorative color accents to charcoal and muted green from plants.
Prioritize child-safe finishes and fabrics — rounded edges, stain-friendly textiles.
Lighting should be layered and dimmable; avoid harsh overhead white light.
negative_prompt: no bright saturated colors, no chrome or mirrored finishes, no cluttered surfaces, no large high-back/overstuffed sofas, no visible baby paraphernalia clutter (except the single staged crib), no cinematic dramatic lighting (keep warm, realistic ambient light), no unrealistic floating furniture or impossible shadows

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Main ambient from diffuse daylight through patio glazing; supplement with a warm (2700K) floor lamp beside sofa and a low table lamp on media credenza. Use soft shadows, no harsh direct beams. Slight backlight from patio to suggest depth.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: View: three-quarter corner shot from near hallway looking into Nest toward patio glazing. Spatial constraints: room ≈11.9 ft x 11.7 ft. Furnish with a low oak-framed linen sofa (neutral off-white), low rectangular coffee table in warm oak, one neutral pouf, textured medium wool rug aligned to sofa and coffee table. Standing_desk: slim, leaning/console standing desk placed in corner adjacent to patio door (against wall) with minimal monitor and a warm task lamp. Add woven toy basket beside sofa and one medium potted fiddle-leaf or ficus near glazing. Materials: light oak, linen, rattan basket, wool rug. Color accents: muted charcoal throw pillow and a small ceramic tray on table. Keep circulation clear to patio door.
room_plans.video_prompt: Slow dolly from hallway into living space, pausing briefly on the standing desk corner, then continuing toward patio to reveal indoor-outdoor connection and low seating composition.
must_avoid: large tall shelving between sofa and patio
oversized sectional
bright-colored textiles

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Soft, even ambient light from window; warm bedside lamps (~2700K) for layered pools. No dramatic spotlights. Keep shadows gentle to preserve restful mood.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: View: eye-level, centered on bed wall showing low platform bed and crib along adjacent wall. Room dimensions ≈12.5 ft x 11.58 ft — allow 30–36 in clearance in front of closet/door. Furnish: low platform bed with warm oak headboard, neutral linen bedding, one low bedside table with a small lamp, soft low-pile rug at foot of bed. Crib: simple wooden crib placed with clearance to allow walkway and access to closet; crib bedding neutral and minimal. Add narrow chest/dresser and one small plant by window. Materials: warm oak, washed linen, wool rug, matte ceramics. Keep accessories minimal: one framed neutral print above bed or a narrow wall shelf.
room_plans.video_prompt: Static slow zoom in from door to bed, pausing to reveal crib placement and dresser, conveying calm and family readiness without clutter.
must_avoid: crib under direct draft from window
oversized tall furniture blocking circulation
busy patterns

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Even, slightly cooler daylight from adjacent dining; add warm under-cabinet task lights to create depth and believable functionality. No strong overhead glare.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: View: angled countertop perspective showing tidy counter, stacked neutral ceramic bowls on open shelf, small potted herb by sink, matte warm-neutral cabinetry and wood-accent handles. Keep counters mostly clear with one small wooden cutting board and a ceramic utensil jar. Visual connection to Dine visible at right edge. Materials: matte lacquer cabinetry, oak shelving, ceramic pieces, stainless sink with matte black faucet accents.
room_plans.video_prompt: Short lateral pan across kitchen counters toward the dining nook, emphasizing tidy staging and material warmth.
must_include: neutral ceramic bowls on open shelf
must_avoid: full countertop of appliances
cluttered utensils

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Single warm (2700K) woven rattan pendant centered over table, dimmable to create intimate glow. Strong daylight from bay windows during daytime shots; balance with soft fill to avoid blown highlights.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: View: slightly elevated 3/4 view into bay dining nook showing round oak table (36–42 in) with 3–4 linen-upholstered chairs. Pendant: woven rattan pendant centered above table. Table staging: one stoneware vase with dried grass stem; two minimal place settings to suggest human scale. Flooring: light oak plank; small neutral rug may sit under table if space allows. Keep circulation paths clear to adjacent kitchen and living areas.
room_plans.video_prompt: Orbital pan circling slowly around table while pendant provides a warm focal glow; finish with a short push toward the window bay to capture natural light.
must_avoid: rectangular or oversized table
over-styled place settings

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Bright natural daylight during day scenes; for evening, warm lantern/strung lights (2700K). Keep shadows soft and even; no harsh specular highlights.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: View: low-angle view from inside looking outward across small covered patio. Furnish with low teak bench or small tea table with floor cushions, woven outdoor rug, one medium potted plant, and a weather-resistant lantern. Materials: teak/treated oak, woven rug, neutral textiles. Keep layout minimal so patio reads as an extension of indoor living.
room_plans.video_prompt: Aerial drift or slight push outward from interior to patio showing seamless indoor-outdoor connection and low-seating tea nook setup.
must_include: low wooden bench or tea table
must_avoid: large outdoor sofas that block opening
too many potted plants crowding the space

warnings: Dining area is small — enforce round table ≤42 in to preserve circulation.
Standing desk location must not block egress to patio; place against wall and keep cable management tidy.
Crib staging must comply with basic safety clearance; avoid mobile or overhead items within reach.
